current_focus: Track a Pareto frontier across reconstruction quality and sparsity/cost. Treat K=128 as one anchor point, not the only success criterion. Prefer experiments that add non-dominated tradeoff points, including smaller-K runs that accept some FVU increase when they improve the overall frontier.
architecture_findings: r30 gated no_change k? -> policy_reject (不允许 no_change；每一轮都必须提出可执行的实验或修复)

failure_patterns.pattern: After two consecutive K128 loss-path probes (`AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625` and `DEAD_FEATURE_THRESHOLD=100000`) both failed to beat the declared `topk/signum/LR=1.6e-3/K=128/EF=12` anchor, the most useful single question is whether the exact reference recipe itself still reproduces a clean late-phase descent under the current stack. This round makes no recipe change; it reruns the fully explicit mainline baseline to refresh the curve-shape reference before any further LR or optimizer decisions.

topk.design_hypothesis: Round 26 showed that disabling `COMPILE_MODEL` cleared the `first_step_timeout` and kept the K128 baseline in the same general FVU band, but with a small regression versus the compiled anchor. The next single question is whether the stabilized non-compiled path prefers a slightly different step size; this round changes only `LR`, lowering it from `1.6e-3` to `1.4e-3` while keeping the rest of the stabilized recipe fixed, to test whether the worse curve came from a mildly over-aggressive learning rate on the uncompiled path.
next_steps: If `LR=1.4e-3` improves the stabilized K128 run, do one more narrow LR refinement on the same runtime path before switching optimizer.
If `LR=1.4e-3` is flat or worse, conclude that the stabilized path does not benefit from a lower step size and move to the optimizer axis next.
If the curve shape improves while final FVU stays similar, use that signal to decide whether runtime stability plus smoother training is worth further work even without a frontier gain.

whitened_topk.design_hypothesis: With the `topk` mainline saturated for five rounds, the highest-value matched architecture probe is a directly compatible near-neighbor that changes support formation without changing the exported reconstruction form. `whitened_topk` keeps the same `K=128` sparse weighted-sum decoder contract as `topk` but adds input preconditioning before top-k competition, so this round asks one clean question: does that architectural change alone produce a meaningfully better curve or enough promise to justify follow-up work.
next_steps: If `whitened_topk` is clearly worse than the stabilized `topk` control, archive it immediately and return to mainline topk recipe work next round.
If `whitened_topk` is roughly competitive with a smoother or still-descending curve, record it as a promising probe result but still return to the topk mainline next round per policy.
If `whitened_topk` beats the matched topk control, schedule exactly one later follow-up on the same family after the mandated return to the mainline.

gated.design_hypothesis: Round 45 showed that the strengthened `gated` recipe (`signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, `USE_HADAMARD=1`) transfers from `K=128` down to `K=96`, improving the prior `gated@96` result while keeping a smooth, still-improving curve. The next single question is whether that gain carries one more step down the cost ladder: if we hold the improved recipe fixed and reduce only `K` to `64`, can `gated` produce a clearly better lower-cost point than the old `K=64` result, or does the Hadamard benefit fade before this support level?
next_steps: If improved `gated@64` clearly beats the old `K=64` result with a healthy curve, continue once more to `K=32` under the same recipe to locate where the transfer breaks.
If `K=64` regresses toward the old control or shows weaker curve quality, stop the downward sweep and treat the improved recipe mainly as a `K=96/128` high-quality lane.

recent_rounds.hypothesis: With `gated` anchored at `K=128, EF=12, optimizer=signum, lr=1.6e-3`, enabling Hadamard input preprocessing may improve conditioning and smooth the training curve enough to outperform the plain baseline; this round asks only whether `use_hadamard=true` helps the `gated` mainline when all recipe variables except preprocessing are held fixed.

The `gated` family may only be competitive at the largest support, so stepping `K` down from `128` to `96` while holding `EF=12`, `optimizer=signum`, and `lr=1.6e-3` tests one question only: does `gated` remain competitive on the adjacent frontier point, or is its apparent edge confined to `K=128`?

Because `gated` beat the matched `topk` control at `K=96`, the next single question is whether that advantage extends one more step down the support ladder; testing `ARCHITECTURE=gated, K=64, EF=12, OPTIMIZER=signum, LR=1.6e-3` will show whether `gated` is broadly stronger across high-K frontier points or only wins near the very top end.

The `gated` mainline has already shown smooth, still-improving curves at `K=128`, `K=96`, and `K=64`, but Round 38 should first fill the missing clean-baseline step in the prescribed order. Re-running the exact `gated` reference recipe with all reference aux/runtime knobs explicitly pinned answers one question only: does the true anchor still reproduce a clean curve under the current stack before we spend another round on lower `K` or renewed recipe tuning?

Round 38 cleanly reproduced the `gated` anchor at `K=128, EF=12, signum, LR=1.6e-3` with the same still-improving late curve, while the only prior LR callback (`1.2e-3`) was uniformly worse. The next single question is therefore whether the current recipe is slightly underdriven rather than overdriven: does a modest LR increase to `2.0e-3` improve the full training trajectory and beat the refreshed anchor?

Round 39 improved the `gated` K128 anchor from `0.149936` to `0.148156` and the whole curve was better from start to finish while still improving late, so the LR branch has not saturated yet. The next single question is whether this gain continues one more small step upward: does `LR=2.4e-3` preserve the healthy curve shape and improve further over the new `2.0e-3` anchor, or does it begin to overdrive the recipe?

Round 40 answered the LR question cleanly: `LR=2.4e-3` preserved a smooth curve but regressed slightly versus the new `2.0e-3` anchor, so the signum LR branch is now sufficiently localized around `2.0e-3`. The next single question in the prescribed order is therefore optimizer: with `ARCHITECTURE=gated, K=128, EF=12, LR=2.0e-3` fixed at the refreshed anchor recipe, does swapping `signum` to `adam` produce a healthier curve or competitive FVU, or does `gated` still clearly prefer `signum`?

Round 41 made the optimizer question clear: `gated+adam` is not merely slightly worse under the updated anchor, it shows the same pathological curve shape as before, with a strong mid-training blow-up and a much worse endpoint than `signum`. With the optimizer branch now effectively closed, the next single question in the prescribed order is loss: under the strengthened `gated` mainline recipe at `K=128, EF=12, signum, LR=2.0e-3`, is the default auxiliary loss actually helping, or does removing it improve the full training trajectory?

Round 42 answered the loss question cleanly: removing the auxiliary term produced an almost identical curve shape and a slightly worse endpoint than the `gated+signum+LR=2.0e-3` anchor, so the loss branch does not look promising. The next single question in the prescribed order is therefore preprocess: with the strengthened `gated` mainline recipe fixed at `K=128, EF=12, signum, LR=2.0e-3`, does enabling Hadamard preprocessing improve conditioning enough to lower the full training curve, or is the plain input path still best?

Round 43 was not a marginal win: enabling Hadamard on the strengthened `gated` anchor improved the full curve from start through end and lowered `K=128` FVU from `0.148156` to `0.142923`, while still improving late. Before treating preprocess as the new operative mainline and moving on to K, the next single question should be a clean confirmation run of the exact Hadamard recipe: does this gain reproduce under the current stack, or was Round 43 an unusually favorable draw?

Round 44 cleanly reproduced the Hadamard-enabled `gated` improvement at `K=128`, so that preprocess gain is now stable enough to treat as the operative recipe rather than a one-off. The next single question is therefore K transfer under the improved recipe: if we keep `gated`, `signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, `EF=12`, and `USE_HADAMARD=1` fixed and step down only to `K=96`, does the stronger recipe preserve or extend `gated`'s advantage at the next lower support where online cost is meaningfully lower?

Round 45 showed that the strengthened `gated` recipe (`signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, `USE_HADAMARD=1`) transfers from `K=128` down to `K=96`, improving the prior `gated@96` result while keeping a smooth, still-improving curve. The next single question is whether that gain carries one more step down the cost ladder: if we hold the improved recipe fixed and reduce only `K` to `64`, can `gated` produce a clearly better lower-cost point than the old `K=64` result, or does the Hadamard benefit fade before this support level?
